rationale: 62 ac of vacant R-3 (Boise Multifamily, MF by-right) on W Victory Rd, ~3.5 mi NW and OUTSIDE the Airport Influence Area — by far the largest by-right multifamily land basis in the radius. No airport residential restriction applies. This is the single most concerning competitive-apartment site by zoning + scale; confirm developability (not gravel-pit/pond or floodway) before sizing, but as zoned it could absorb a very large apartment program.

~22.8 ac vacant under an SP-01 Specific Plan on E Parkcenter Blvd, ~2.2 mi N and outside the AIA. Specific-Plan entitlement is negotiated — residential density depends on the adopted plan — so the MF yield is Unknown but the size, river-adjacent Parkcenter location and lack of airport restriction make it a real medium-term multifamily/mixed-use supply candidate. Pull the SP-01 plan to confirm permitted residential.

6.3 ac vacant MX-3 (Mixed-Use Regional, MF by-right) at 300 E Parkcenter Blvd, ~4.2 mi N, outside the AIA, in the established Parkcenter office/residential node by the Boise River. By-right multifamily with services in place — a credible competitive apartment site (paired with the 2.8-ac MX-3 at 292 W Parkcenter below).

~10.2 ac vacant R-3 (Boise Multifamily) near 1507 S Oakland Ave, ~4.5 mi NW toward the Vista/Overland corridor, outside the AIA. By-right MF basis; farther submarket and edge of the 5-mi ring, so a secondary but real competitor.
2.8 ac vacant MX-3 at 292 W Parkcenter Blvd, ~4.3 mi N, outside the AIA — same by-right mixed-use node as #3; smaller, so medium concern. Likely develops as mixed-use with a residential component.

~7.6 ac vacant R-3 at 1550 S Loggers Pond Pl, ~3.8 mi NW, outside the AIA. By-right MF zoning, but the 'Loggers Pond' location is in the Boise River gravel-pit/pond belt — a meaningful share may be water/HOA open space rather than buildable. Medium pending a developability check.

~5.3 ac vacant R-2 (Two-Family/Low-Density, MF conditional) at 1198 E River Run Dr, ~3.4 mi N along the Boise River. Lower-density residential basis; plausible townhome/small-MF infill in an amenity location, outside the AIA.

~8.8 ac vacant R-2 near W Hillcrest Ln, ~4.1 mi W (Vista/Hillcrest area), outside the AIA. Low-density-residential by-right; townhome/duplex or low-rise potential. Edge-of-ring, different submarket from the subject.

zoning_plain: Boise Mixed-Use Neighborhood (Ada County/Boise); Specific Plan SP-02 (Ada County/Boise)
rationale: ~8.9 ac vacant split MX-1 / A on E Parkcenter Blvd, ~2.5 mi N, outside the AIA. Mixed-Use Neighborhood allows residential at moderate density; partly open-land. A medium mixed-use/MF watch site in the Parkcenter/Micron-adjacent area.
~3.2 ac vacant R-3 at 281 E Fairbrook Dr, ~2.6 mi N, outside the AIA. By-right MF but small and likely a remnant/infill parcel inside an existing subdivision; modest competitive weight.

12.6 ac vacant MX-2 (would be MF by-right) at 7983 S Federal Way, only 0.84 mi from the subject — but it sits in Airport Influence Area ZONE B, where NEW residential is PROHIBITED. Despite the MF-friendly zoning it is not a credible apartment-supply threat; it will develop as airport-adjacent commercial/industrial (Micron/airport support, logistics). Documents the central finding: the airport, not zoning, governs supply here.

~7 ac vacant MX-2 at 6650 S Eisenman Rd, 0.6 mi from the subject, in AIA Zone B (new residential prohibited). MF zoning is moot; this is industrial/logistics land in the Gowen/Eisenman corridor (Boise Gateway / Pleasant Valley context). Not an apartment threat.

~2.2 ac vacant MX-2 at 2149 E Hospitality Ln, 0.34 mi from the subject, AIA Zone B. Closest vacant 'MF-zoned' parcel to Canyon Ridge, but residential is prohibited by the airport overlay — commercial/hospitality infill only. Reinforces that the subject's immediate surroundings cannot add competing apartments.

~2.1 ac vacant MX-2 at 1915 W Airport Way, ~3.2 mi W on the airport's north side, AIA Zone B. MF-zoned on paper, residential prohibited in practice; airport commercial/industrial. Listed to document the pattern across the airport frontage.
